_note: Fictional clause library. 25 short precedent extracts across NDA, M&A, employment, commercial, real estate, finance, IP and DP. 'Slant' indicates whether the clause favours the buyer/seller/employer/landlord/etc. or is neutral.

precedents.title: Mutual NDA - Confidential Information definition (2026 update)
precedents.body: 'Confidential Information' means any information disclosed by either party in connection with the Permitted Purpose, in any form, that is identified as confidential at the time of disclosure or that a reasonable person would understand to be confidential. It does not include information that (i) is or becomes publicly available without breach of this agreement; (ii) was lawfully known to the receiving party prior to disclosure; (iii) is independently developed without reference to the disclosed information; or (iv) is rightfully obtained from a third party without breach of any duty of confidentiality. For the avoidance of doubt, no information shall be excluded by reason only that it can be reverse-engineered from publicly available materials.

precedents.title: NDA - AI tools restriction
precedents.body: The receiving party shall not input any Confidential Information into any third-party AI, machine-learning or large-language-model system except (a) services explicitly listed in Schedule 2; or (b) services where the receiving party has obtained a binding written commitment from the provider that inputs will not be used for model training and will be processed in a closed-tenant environment. Any other use shall be a material breach of this agreement.

precedents.title: SPA - Limitation of liability cap (seller-friendly)
precedents.body: Save in respect of the Fundamental Warranties and any claim arising from fraud, the maximum aggregate liability of the Sellers in respect of all Claims under this Agreement shall not exceed 50% of the Consideration actually received by the Sellers. Liability for breach of the Tax Warranties shall not exceed 100% of the Consideration.

precedents.title: SPA - Disclosure regime (buyer-friendly)
precedents.body: Disclosures shall be effective only to the extent that the matter is fairly disclosed in the Disclosure Letter with sufficient detail to enable a reasonable purchaser to identify the nature, scope and quantum of the matter disclosed. General disclosures of the Data Room shall not constitute Fair Disclosure.

precedents.title: SHA - Drag-along (standard)
precedents.body: If shareholders holding not less than 70% of the Ordinary Shares ('Dragging Shareholders') wish to transfer all of their shares to a bona fide arm's-length third party purchaser, they may serve a Drag Notice on the remaining shareholders requiring them to transfer all of their shares on the same terms (subject to the limitations in clause [X]).

precedents.title: SHA - Tag-along (minority-friendly)
precedents.body: Any proposed transfer of shares carrying more than 30% of the voting rights to a third party shall be conditional upon the proposed transferee offering to acquire from each other shareholder, on the same terms and at the same price per share, all (and not some only) of the shares held by such shareholder.

precedents.title: Employment contract - Non-compete (12 months)
precedents.body: The Employee shall not, for a period of 12 months following the Termination Date, either alone or in conjunction with any other person, directly or indirectly, be engaged in or concerned with any business which competes with the Restricted Business in the Restricted Territory. The Employee acknowledges that this restriction is reasonable having regard to the Employee's seniority and access to confidential information.

precedents.title: Employment - Notice period (mid-level)
precedents.body: Either party may terminate this Agreement on six months' written notice. The Employer may make a payment in lieu of notice equal to the Employee's basic salary for the unworked portion of the notice period and any contractual benefits as set out in Schedule 1.

precedents.title: Settlement agreement - Waiver of statutory claims
precedents.body: The Employee waives all claims, whether known or unknown, arising out of or in connection with the Employee's employment or its termination, including (without limitation) claims listed in Schedule 1 (which contains the specific statutory claims required for the waiver to be effective under section 147 of the Equality Act 2010 and section 203 of the Employment Rights Act 1996).

precedents.title: Commercial - Limitation of liability (supplier-friendly)
precedents.body: Subject to clause [X] (Unlimited Liability), the Supplier's total aggregate liability arising out of or in connection with this Agreement shall not exceed 125% of the Charges paid or payable in the 12 months preceding the event giving rise to liability, and the Supplier shall not be liable for any indirect, consequential or special losses, loss of profits, loss of revenue, loss of anticipated savings or loss of goodwill.

precedents.title: Commercial - Termination for change of control
precedents.body: Either party may terminate this Agreement on 30 days' written notice if there is a Change of Control of the other party. 'Change of Control' has the meaning given in section 1124 of the Corporation Tax Act 2010, and includes any transfer of the relevant business or substantially all of its assets.

precedents.title: SaaS - Data portability on exit
precedents.body: On termination or expiry, the Supplier shall, at no additional cost, provide the Customer's Data in a structured, machine-readable, commonly used format (and in any open standard format if requested) within 30 days. The Supplier shall provide reasonable transition assistance for a further 90 days at the Supplier's then current rates.

precedents.title: Lease - Break clause (tenant-only, rolling)
precedents.body: The Tenant may terminate this lease on the third anniversary of the term commencement date or any subsequent anniversary by giving the Landlord not less than six months' prior written notice. The break shall be conditional only on payment of all rents up to the break date and giving back vacant possession (free of underleases and licences). No further conditions shall apply.

precedents.title: Lease - Rent review (RPI capped and collared)
precedents.body: The Annual Rent shall be reviewed on each Review Date by reference to the increase in the All Items Retail Prices Index since the immediately preceding Review Date (or the Term Commencement Date if no Review Date has occurred). Each review shall be subject to a minimum (collar) of 1% and a maximum (cap) of 4% per annum, compounded annually.

precedents.title: Lease - Alienation (tenant-friendly)
precedents.body: The Tenant may, with the prior written consent of the Landlord (such consent not to be unreasonably withheld or delayed), assign or underlet the whole of the Premises to a Permitted Assignee or Permitted Undertenant. The Landlord shall not require an authorised guarantee agreement save in circumstances permitted under section 19(1A) of the Landlord and Tenant Act 1927.

precedents.title: Facility - Material adverse change (lender-friendly)
precedents.body: An Event of Default shall occur if any event or circumstance occurs which, in the reasonable opinion of the Majority Lenders, has had or is reasonably likely to have a Material Adverse Effect on (a) the business, operations, assets or financial condition of the Group taken as a whole; (b) the ability of any Obligor to perform its payment obligations under the Finance Documents; or (c) the validity or enforceability of any Finance Document or any rights or remedies of any Finance Party thereunder.

precedents.title: Facility - Prepayment on change of control
precedents.body: If a Change of Control occurs, the Borrower shall promptly notify the Agent and, if required by any Lender by notice given within 30 days of such notification, the Loans of that Lender shall become immediately due and payable together with all accrued interest, fees and other amounts.

precedents.title: Debenture - Negative pledge (standard)
precedents.body: The Chargor shall not, without the prior written consent of the Security Agent, create or permit to subsist any Security over the whole or any part of the Charged Property, save for Permitted Security as defined in the Facility Agreement.

precedents.title: IP assignment - Present assignment of future IPRs
precedents.body: The Assignor hereby assigns to the Assignee, by way of present assignment of present and future copyright (and other intellectual property rights), all Intellectual Property Rights subsisting now or in future in the Works, with full title guarantee and free from all Encumbrances. To the extent any such rights vest in the Assignor only when they come into existence, the Assignor hereby assigns them to the Assignee with effect from the moment of vesting.

precedents.title: IP - Moral rights waiver
precedents.body: To the extent permitted by law, the Assignor irrevocably and unconditionally waives all moral rights (including under sections 77 to 85 of the Copyright, Designs and Patents Act 1988) to which the Assignor is now or may become entitled in respect of the Works, in favour of the Assignee, its successors and licensees.

precedents.title: Trade mark co-existence - Field-of-use restriction
precedents.body: Each party shall use its respective Marks only in the relevant Field of Use set out in Schedule 1 and shall not (a) use, register or apply to register any sign confusingly similar to the other party's Marks; (b) challenge the validity or ownership of the other party's Marks; or (c) take any action reasonably likely to harm the goodwill associated with the other party's Marks.

precedents.title: DPA - International transfer mechanism
precedents.body: Where the Processor or any Sub-processor processes Personal Data outside the United Kingdom or the EEA, the parties shall ensure that an appropriate transfer mechanism is in place, such as the UK International Data Transfer Addendum or the EU Standard Contractual Clauses (in each case as updated from time to time). The Processor shall conduct and document a transfer impact assessment in respect of each such transfer.

precedents.title: DPA - Sub-processor consent
precedents.body: The Processor shall not engage any Sub-processor without the Controller's prior written consent. The Processor shall give the Controller not less than 30 days' prior written notice of any proposed change to its Sub-processors. The Controller may object to any proposed Sub-processor on reasonable grounds, in which case the parties shall work together in good faith to find a mutually acceptable alternative.

precedents.title: Boilerplate - Entire agreement (with carve-out for fraud)
precedents.body: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties and supersedes all prior agreements, representations and understandings (whether written or oral). Each party acknowledges that, in entering into this Agreement, it has not relied on, and shall have no remedy in respect of, any statement, representation or warranty (whether negligently or innocently made) other than as expressly set out in this Agreement. Nothing in this clause shall limit or exclude liability for fraud or fraudulent misrepresentation.

precedents.title: Boilerplate - Governing law and jurisdiction (English)
precedents.body: This Agreement and any dispute or claim (including non-contractual disputes or claims) arising out of or in connection with it or its subject matter or formation shall be governed by, and construed in accordance with, the law of England and Wales. The parties irrevocably agree that the courts of England and Wales shall have exclusive jurisdiction.
